findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/LocallyContractible.lean:83-85` defines `LocallyContractibleSpace`; `:94-95` says strong local contractibility is strictly stronger; `:173-174` proves `StronglyLocallyContractibleSpace.locallyContractible`.
findings.fix: Add a theorem `SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace.of_locallyContractibleSpace (h : LocallyContractibleSpace X) : SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X` and derive the strong instance from `StronglyLocallyContractibleSpace.locallyContractible`.
findings.issue: The semilocal simple-connectivity consequence is proved only from `[StronglyLocallyContractibleSpace X]`, but Mathlib has the weaker natural hypothesis `LocallyContractibleSpace X`, whose definition is exactly null-homotopic inclusions of small neighborhoods.

summary: The new predicate is at the right level, but the local-contractibility API is under-generalized: it stops at the stronger Mathlib class instead of using the weaker classical hypothesis already available.
